pericopes.pericopeTitle: [JERUSALEM TO BE DESTROYED]JEREMIAH’S IMPRISONMENT AND RELEASE

entries.author: St. Basil the Great
entries.title: GOD BRINGS LOW, SUSTAINS AND UPLIFTS.
entries.body: Thus there is the expression in Exodus: From the firstborn of Pharaoh to the firstborn of the captive woman who was in the pit.{fn:0} But they even threw Jeremiah also into a pit. His brothers through jealousy confined Joseph in a pit without water.{fn:1} Each act, therefore, either draws us downward by oppressing us with sin or lifts us upward by raising us on wings toward God. Therefore, you have saved me, I who formerly lived a wicked life, and have separated me from those who go down to the dark and frigid region. The meaning of the words you have upheld me is to say, you have led me back from my downward course, so as not to give my enemies an occasion to rejoice over me. {fn:2}

entries.author: St. Ambrose of Milan
entries.title: JEREMIAH, DANIEL BROUGHT THE PEOPLE COMFORT.
entries.body: One could judge that merit and virtue could be evident even in captivity. For Jeremiah was not less happy in captivity, nor was Daniel{fn:0} or Esdras,{fn:1} nor were Anania and Azaria and Misael less happy than if they had not fallen into captivity.{fn:2} They entered into captivity in such a way that they brought to their people both present consolations in captivity and the hope of escaping from it.{fn:3} {fn:4}

entries.author: Theodoret of Cyr
entries.title: JEREMIAH RELEASED.
entries.body: Jeremiah said, Now that matters have proven the truthfulness of my prophecy and the falsehood of your prophets, you must now judge rightly and punish those who lied, not him who warned you of the truth beforehand. Then he asked to be released from the house of the scribe. {fn:0}
